titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/107

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=389 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:36:20.452248+00 | translation moa=390 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:36:22.367529+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis tam futuris quam presentibus Ricardus de Morevill' Constabularius regis Scott' salutem .
Sciatis me dedisse . et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Mailros et monachis
ibidem deo seruientibus in liberam et quietam et perpetuam elemosinam . libertatem et licentiam habendi apud
buchelm unam uaccariam sexaginta uaccarum . et domum ad opus earum ideoneam  infra illa eadem
septa que ibi erant antequam hanc licentiam eis dedissem . Quare uolo ut prenominata ecclesia sancte Ma
rie de Mailros et predicti monachi prefatam libertatem et licentiam in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam
habeant . perpetualiter que possideant . Ita libere . quiete . et honorifice  sicut aliquam elemosinam liberius
quietius et honorificentius possident . Ita quod hoc non remaneat propter cartam meam de Þhiteleie
quam prius eis dederam . que testatur me concessisse eis ut habeant solummodo tres domos in
foresta  et hoc apud predictam Þhiteleie ; Teste Domina auice de loncastria . Willelmo de Moreuill'
filio et herede nostro . herberto decano Glasg' . magistro Clemente capellano . Adam persona ecclesie de
laweder . Rogero clerico et dapifero . Alano de turlestan . Sampsone filio Willelmi de Vlchilestun .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church both future and present, Richard de Morville, constable of the king of the Scots, sends greeting. Know me to have given, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and the monks serving God in that place, in free and quit and everlasting alms, the freedom and licence to have at Buckholm one cow pasture for sixty cows, and a suitable building for their needs, within the same enclosure which was there before I had given them this permission. Wherefore I will that the aforenamed church of St Mary at Melrose and the foresaid monks should have the forementioned liberty and licence in free and everlasting alms, and possess it for ever; as freely, quitely, and honourably as they possess any alms freely, quitely, and honourably; so that it shall non remain a hindrance on account of my charter for Whitelee which I had given to them previously, which shows me to have granted to them that they may have only three buildings in the forest, and that at the foresaid Whitelee. As witness: the lady Avice of Lancaster; William de Morville our son and heir; Herbert the dean of Glasgow; Master Clement, chaplain; Adam, parson of the church of Lauder; Roger clerk and steward; Alan of Thirlestane; Sampson son of William of Oxton.
